Question: Across the following four specific wetland records — (1) RecordKey "Donana-Marsh-10 lagoonal reedbed research node" with EcoHydroFacet "lacustrine fringe marsh with fetch-limited littoral zone", VegetationStratum "Typha angustifolia emergent belt with helophyte understory", and HydraulicExchangeIndex "surface crust constrained connectivity with biocrust impermeable cap"; (2) RecordKey "Forsinard-FT-001 flux tower long-term monitoring" with EcoHydroFacet "tidal brackish mangrove fringe with pneumatophore networks", VegetationStratum "Typha angustifolia emergent belt with helophyte understory", and HydraulicExchangeIndex "high-frequency overwash connectivity associated with storm surge breaching"; (3) RecordKey "Biebrza-Wet-09 alluvial marsh reference plot" with EcoHydroFacet "fluvial overbank reedbed corridor with seasonal sedimentation", VegetationStratum "Rhizophora mangrove prop-root structural assemblage", and HydraulicExchangeIndex "diffuse subsurface seepage-driven connectivity inferred from piezometers"; and (4) RecordKey "TonleSap-Floor-05 seasonal inundation benchmark" with EcoHydroFacet "lacustrine fringe marsh with fetch-limited littoral zone", VegetationStratum "Betula pubescens scrubby carr with alder encroachment", and HydraulicExchangeIndex "artificially regulated hydraulic regime via sluice and culvert operations" — what is the total SurfaceCO2Efflux (sum of SurfaceCO2Efflux) when these individual measurements are combined?

Question: What is the combined total yearly microbial respiration due to decomposition across these four peatland settings: the perched hollow central site experiencing periodic flooding from river backflows with iron-associated organic particle transport and slow-decaying dwarf shrub litter; the rewetted farm peatland with a persistent moisture fringed zone supporting methane-producing microbes, where organic matter seeps into intertwined stream channels from banks and woody plants are in a moderate stage of decay releasing lignin-rich detritus; and finally, at the coastal peat tongue, where a long water retention period under low evaporation feeds two different organic carbon transport pathways—one through underground fen transition water flow associated with a persistent sedge mat low in nitrogen, and the other via surface runoff through Sphagnum-based litter allowing fen sedge with irregular senescence waves?

Question: What is the combined total of methane emissions measured at four particular peat sampling sites, each exhibiting distinct microbial methane production states—one with enhanced cooperation leading to faster acetate breakdown in a peat mound isolated from lateral water movement; another showing reduced methane output due to sulfate presence and electron acceptor competition in a coastal area influenced by tidal saltwater pulses; a third where plant-mediated gas transport boosts bubbling emissions on a plateau elevated above the regional groundwater; and a fourth limited by organic substrate availability in a slack area with accumulated peat?
SQL Query: SELECT SUM(CH4FluxRate) AS CH4FluxRate FROM (SELECT CH4FluxRate AS CH4FluxRate FROM PeatlandMethanogenesisRegister WHERE AnoxicPeatSequenceID = "APS-Temperate-PLT-004-plateau core downstream of artificial drainage" AND MethanogenesisPhenostate = "syntrophic consortia prevalence with accelerated acetate oxidation rates" AND HydraulicConnectivityClass = "isolated hummock with minimal lateral solute exchange and perched water table" UNION ALL SELECT CH4FluxRate AS CH4FluxRate FROM PeatlandMethanogenesisRegister WHERE AnoxicPeatSequenceID = "APS-SubArctic-FEN-003-sequence sampled adjacent to minerotrophic seep" AND MethanogenesisPhenostate = "suppressed methanogenesis due to sulfate intrusion and electron acceptor competition" AND HydraulicConnectivityClass = "tidally influenced coastal peat with saline intrusion pulses and periodic flushing" UNION ALL SELECT CH4FluxRate AS CH4FluxRate FROM PeatlandMethanogenesisRegister WHERE AnoxicPeatSequenceID = "APS-Coastal-TID-005-sequence influenced by episodic saline inundation" AND MethanogenesisPhenostate = "plant-mediated transport enhancing ebullitive pulses via graminoid aerenchyma" AND HydraulicConnectivityClass = "ombrotrophic plateau perched above regional water table with capillary fringe" UNION ALL SELECT CH4FluxRate AS CH4FluxRate FROM PeatlandMethanogenesisRegister WHERE AnoxicPeatSequenceID = "APS-Alpine-SLK-006-sequence from slack with peat accumulation pulse" AND MethanogenesisPhenostate = "substrate-limited hydrogenotrophic steady-state with low organic substrate availability" AND HydraulicConnectivityClass = "ombrotrophic plateau perched above regional water table with capillary fringe");

Question: What is the combined carbon stock, measured in megagrams per hectare, found in the specific wetland areas consisting of a 2018-established mangrove fringe dominated by cordgrass on a high-tide platform with underlying peat and characterized by sulfur-rich, oxygen-deprived soil layers smelling of hydrogen sulfide; the same mangrove site but featuring salt flats with lingering cyanobacteria and mineral crusts exhibiting iron-reducing microzones near roots; plus a backbarrier zone dominated by algae mats with ephemeral flatlands transitioning to sedge fens on silty alluvial soils showing an oxygen-rich gradient near root zones; and also nearby brackish rush wetlands interlaced with rhizome peat deposits in methane-emitting reduced soil layers enriched in dissolved organic compounds?

Question: What is the combined total of carbon dioxide emissions measured from these three specific survey records: (1) the "Southwest tidal creek mouth with episodic scour and sand ribbons" during the "dry-season follow-up 2021 — biomass senescence window" in an "intertidal mudflat with persistent microphytobenthos and EPS crusts"; (2) the "Barrier flat behind dune ridge with bioturbated peat lenses" during the "post-monsoon rapid-response 2017 — rainfall-influenced sampling" in an "estuarine sand-silt interface with mixed siliciclastic-organic layering"; and (3) the "Inner estuary backbarrier composed of fine-grained anoxic sulphidic mud" during the "baseline campaign 2016 — synoptic low-tide survey" in a "brackish embayment mud with sulfide-enriched anoxic horizons"?
SQL Query: SELECT SUM(CarbonVentFlux) AS CarbonVentFlux FROM (SELECT CarbonVentFlux AS CarbonVentFlux FROM BenthicCarbonEvasionSurvey WHERE SurveyQuadrant = "Southwest tidal creek mouth with episodic scour and sand ribbons" AND MonitoringRound = "dry-season follow-up 2021 — biomass senescence window" AND EcosystemNexus = "intertidal mudflat with persistent microphytobenthos and EPS crusts" UNION ALL SELECT CarbonVentFlux AS CarbonVentFlux FROM BenthicCarbonEvasionSurvey WHERE SurveyQuadrant = "Barrier flat behind dune ridge with bioturbated peat lenses" AND MonitoringRound = "post-monsoon rapid-response 2017 — rainfall-influenced sampling" AND EcosystemNexus = "estuarine sand-silt interface with mixed siliciclastic-organic layering" UNION ALL SELECT CarbonVentFlux AS CarbonVentFlux FROM BenthicCarbonEvasionSurvey WHERE SurveyQuadrant = "Inner estuary backbarrier composed of fine-grained anoxic sulphidic mud" AND MonitoringRound = "baseline campaign 2016 — synoptic low-tide survey" AND EcosystemNexus = "brackish embayment mud with sulfide-enriched anoxic horizons");

Question: What is the combined total of greenhouse gas emissions measured across the four distinct monitoring sites—Deltaic estuarine flux tower 07 for post-wildfire organic oxidation CO2 during the 2022-2023 water year in boreal peat bogs; Permafrost polygon eddy array alpha for bioturbation-influenced CO2 microhotspots recorded in the 2017-2018 hydrological year within deltaic intertidal sediments; Alpine soil chamber cluster west ridge for similar bioturbation-boosted CO2 fluxes during 2016-2017 in a periglacial polygon field; and Arid shrubland flux sentinel 12 for methane bubbling from waterlogged fens observed in the 2017-2018 period in the same periglacial polygons?
